myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, the world was silent, save for the wind brushing against the mountains. In a deep valley lived a nymph named Aethel, whose voice was so beautiful it caused the flowers to bloom and the rivers to dance. The gods of the sky grew envious of her gift, for they had no such music in their celestial halls. 

One day, the God of Storms descended, demanding Aethel sing only for him. She refused, stating her voice belonged to the earth and the creatures who dwelled upon it. Infuriated by her defiance, the storm god struck the valley with a blinding bolt of lightning, shattering the nymph into a thousand shimmering fragments of sound. 

But Aethel’s spirit could not be destroyed. Instead of fading, she bonded with the very stones of the mountains and the walls of the caves. Now, whenever a traveler calls out into the valleys, Aethel answers. She no longer sings her own songs, but she faithfully repeats the words of others, ensuring that the world will never return to its ancient, lonely silence. Her voice remains, a timeless reminder of the nymph who chose the earth over the heavens.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, before humans walked the earth, the jagged peaks of Mount Oakhaven were home to a solitary spirit named Elyria. Woven from mist and stone, Elyria possessed a voice of unparalleled beauty, yet she had no one to share it with. Each twilight, she would sing melancholic melodies that drifted aimlessly into the deep ravines, swallowed by the vast, uncaring silence. 

The North Wind, a restless and wandering entity named Boreas, heard her sorrowful songs during his travels. Touched by her profound loneliness, Boreas decided to give her a gift. He swept through the canyons, gathering the fragmented notes of her song, and rushed back to the mountain's peak. As Elyria sang her final note, Boreas blew the gathered sounds back toward her in a rushing breeze. 

Startled, Elyria heard her own voice return, softer and slightly delayed, as if a hidden sibling spirit were answering her call. Overjoyed, she sang louder, and the Wind threw the notes back again and again. 

To this day, when you stand upon a high peak and shout into the void, Boreas still honors his ancient pact, carrying your voice back to you so that no one in the mountains ever has to feel truly alone. Thus, the Echo was born.

To this day, Elyria’s spirit remains bonded to the stone. When a traveler calls out into the void, Boreas rushes past, stirring her resting fragments. She answers back, a timeless reminder of the mountain spirit who defied the storm to ensure the world would never be truly lonely.
